reviews.review_msg: John is not that bad. The course is outlined clearly with lots of resources. Workload is reasonable. Discussion posts are easy and only a few sentences. Longer written assignment not that bad (two reflection papers and one policy). He replaces the quiz grade with the midterm if u did better. Reviews are right that course mostly focuses on vehicles

nice prof, he cares about class feedback. his lectures were a bit long so i skipped a couple, but found the class to be overall interesting and didn't find it very time consuming to catch up on concepts through the slides. he's pretty clear on what you need to know for the exams so they were pretty straightforward, same with assignments.

i found the topic interesting but his lectures are a bit dry. he has a great canvas set up: clear outline of what topics you should know after each week with optional practice quizzes. makes it super easy to study for the exams, which i found to be fairly straight forward. overall not bad, i think the other reviews are a bit harsh.

He is a fine professor, tests and assignments are all fair. Seems like a genuine guy and cares a lot about feedback on the course, which is nice. Giving 3 stars because he rambles like crazy and is so hard to follow along with in lectures.

Maybe his other classes are alright. This class was crazy lecture-heavy, and I felt like I didn't really learn much. His exams were fair: MC, short answer, and long answer; however, every lecture felt like I had to go over them all again (which I didn't) because it drained me so much;. Honestly, it ruined my semester.
Lets just say he got reported to the dean

Loves to ramble about things while at the same time not explaining things clearly. Very test heavy and lectures are very boring. He is allotted 3 hour lectures and somehow manage to not finish his materials in that 3 hours. Only take the course if you have extra time in your week to sit without any purpose for 3 hours.

Easily the worst prof I have encountered at SFU. He is absolutely no help, has a pissy attitude, and reads off the slides. Horrendous job connecting ideas and concepts. Don't expect any characteristics that makes a teacher a good teacher.

Don't take this class unless you love talking about electric cars for 3 hours, not having your questions answered properly, or love being ignored by your prof. Have a concession or family issue? Jonn doesn't care. Have a relevant in question lecture? He won't answer it and will make you feel dumb.

Irritable. dense lectures that leave you with nothing. no support. cannot answer questions adequately. our TA saved this class, love her. she marked all assignments, exams quizzes etc. lecture is 3 hours and he goes straight to the last minute. lectures have no direction and he rambles about electric cars. 1 star.

THIS PROF HAS ANGER ISSUES witnessed him get mad at IT guy over a microphone. He is not very personable to say the least. Lectures are very dense w/ too many graphs and details hard to channel focus of lecture. Assignments poorly spaced out - in three weeks we had a midterm, group project, and paper due. Professor uses mostly own work for material

seems nice enough, but BEWARE 355 is ENTIRELY about electric vehicles, name of course is very misleading. Zero material on urban and transit topics. all readings provided written by prof as well which felt restricting. Content felt contradictory as we're talking about sustainable options, yet alluded towards everyone buying an EV. sustainable!?

Axsen is great the class was something that i really enjoyed. There was lots to cover so lectures feel dense in material. I did not like having to do a final paper and a final exam just pick one please. Other then that I would highly recommend REM 355.

Good professor. Lectures are a bit dull but the material is interesting. Fair midterm and final. Marking was also fair by the TA. Easy paper and group project. Lots of easy marks for participation, journal entries and discussion board. Not sure how he is through email as I've only contacted the TA through there. Seems like a good guy though.

Monotone lectures, and emailing him his tone in said email comes off as not caring. Won't easily accommodate for things out of your control. Lectures are very heavy on content and builds up on one another. I learned more on YouTube than I did his lectures.
